summary: Single-storey rectangular pavilion with stepped oak plank gable-like roof, stone-brick lower walls, a white smooth border plinth and continuous row of windows on each long side. Building sits centered inside a shallow recessed dirt perimeter.

shape.overall_form: simple rectangular box with a symmetrical stepped roof running along the long axis
shape.symmetry: bilaterally symmetrical along the roof ridge (longitudinal symmetry)

elements: continuous horizontal row of glass windows along each long side (4-5 windows per side)
raised inner wooden floor/plinth visible at base of walls
stepped layered oak plank roof with stone slab end-caps
white smooth border (one block wide) around building perimeter at ground level
stonebrick lower wall band and occasional cobblestone corner blocks
rebuild_hints: Footprint is rectangular with a 1-block white smooth border around the base; build inner floor one block inset from that border.
Lower wall: one to two block high wood plank (base), then ~2 blocks of stonebrick with glass windows inserted in the middle band.
Roof: centered ridge along long axis using oak planks laid as horizontal stepped layers descending to each side; place stone slabs or similar at each step's end as caps.
Place cobblestone or textured block at short-side corners as vertical accents.
Windows are narrow horizontal panes separated by 1-block stonebrick columns; repeat evenly along each long side.
uncertainties: Exact material for the white border is ambiguous (quartz, white concrete, or smooth stone); labeled as smooth_quartz_or_concrete with medium confidence.
Precise block types for roof caps and small decorative slabs are uncertain (stone slab vs smooth stone slab).
Internal layout, floor height, and presence of doors/entrances are not visible from provided views; entrance location and interior details are unknown.
Exact footprint dimensions estimated from pixel views; actual block counts may differ by a few blocks.
